About

I'm a data analyst and Georgia Tech OMSA candidate based in Atlanta. My work sits at the intersection of statistics, engineering, and product thinking — building forecasting models, analytics pipelines, and the interfaces that help teams act on what the data is saying.

Before joining Axiom Analytics, I worked at Meridian Group where I built self-serve reporting infrastructure for the operations team. I hold a BS in Statistics and am completing my MS in Analytics (OMSA) part-time. Outside of work I write about Bayesian inference, reproducible research, and data tooling.

Experience

  1. June 2023 – Present Atlanta, GA (Remote)

    Senior Data Analyst

    Axiom Analytics

    • Automated a 6-hour manual ETL process into a 20-minute dbt pipeline, freeing the analytics team for higher-value modeling work.
    • Built a self-serve Looker dashboard suite adopted by three product teams, reducing ad-hoc SQL requests by 40%.
    • Partnered with ML engineering to instrument feature pipelines for a churn prediction model, contributing to a 15% uplift in 90-day retention.
    • Designed and enforced a dbt testing standard across 80+ models — null checks, referential integrity, accepted-value assertions — catching data quality regressions before they reached dashboards.
    • Mentored two junior analysts on query optimization, reproducible analysis workflows, and presentation of statistical findings to non-technical stakeholders.
    SQLdbtLookerLookMLPythonBigQuerySnowflakeGit
  2. May 2021 – June 2023 Atlanta, GA

    Data Analyst

    Meridian Consulting Group

    • Delivered a market-sizing analysis for a Fortune 500 retail client, combining POS transaction data with census data to identify a $12M regional expansion opportunity.
    • Maintained and extended a Tableau reporting suite covering KPIs for eight client accounts, cutting weekly report generation time from 4 hours to 30 minutes via parameter-driven templates.
    • Wrote Python scripts to clean and deduplicate CRM exports, reducing data quality incidents flagged by account managers by 60%.
    • Contributed to a pricing elasticity model in R that informed a 7% price increase on two SKU families with projected revenue impact of $2.4M annually.
    SQLPythonRTableauExcelSalesforce CRM
